design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling island stages with hazards, collect power-ups and dinosaur allies, reach boss encounter, defeat boss to progress to next island
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited lives, inventory management for dinosaur allies and hammers) combined with stage hazards forcing careful traversal and strategic ally selection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master Higgins rescuing his kidnapped girlfriend Tina across eight perilous islands with the aid of four unique dinosaur companions, each with distinct traversal and combat abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentionally removed mid-stage checkpoints despite shorter stages, forcing mastery through repetition. Inventory-based dinosaur ally system creates pre-stage strategic choice—players must commit to specific companion loadouts rather than adapting mid-level. Boss relocation mechanic (boss moves if defeated mid-fight) discourages repeated attempts and adds penalty for failed encounters

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with stage restart on death; no mid-stage checkpoints despite shorter level design
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system eliminated from original game. Players must restart stage from beginning on death, but stages are significantly shorter than predecessor to compensate
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through pre-stage inventory selection: players choose which dinosaur ally to bring and whether to equip stone hammers, directly affecting stage traversal capabilities and combat options. Backtracking allowed within stages (to a certain point) provides exploration-based difficulty mitigation

progression.upgrade_system: Inventory-based system allowing accumulation of stone hammers (no longer upgrade to fireballs; instead added to reserved inventory) and four dinosaur ally types with distinct abilities. Each dinosaur summoned via specific playing card suit hidden in eggs: heart (blue camptosaurus, ice-walking), spade (red camptosaurus, fire-breathing), clover (pteranodon, flight), diamond (elasmosaurus, underwater breathing)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Dinosaur allies unlocked by collecting card suits hidden within eggs throughout stages. Keys accessed from hidden eggs unlock bonus zones and shortcuts to subsequent islands. Progression gated by boss defeats on each of eight islands
replayability_hooks: Four distinct dinosaur allies encourage replaying stages with different companion selections for varied traversal strategies
Hidden eggs with different rewards (bonus points via spinning egg roulette at stage completion) incentivize thorough exploration
Shortcut access via key-based bonus zones allows speedrun-like replaying with alternate routes
Underwater and vertical-scrolling stage variety presents different mechanical challenges across islands

world_building_techniques: Opening premise establishes clear rescue narrative (Tina kidnapped by Evil Witch Doctor's followers)
Eight perilous islands controlled by monster minions serve as environmental context for progression structure
Dinosaur allies framed as 'friendly' creatures opposing common oppressors, suggesting environmental cooperation narrative
Stage-based progression across islands implies exploration of distinct themed locations

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as island gatekeepers requiring strategy beyond stage hazards. Relocation mechanic (boss moves to another stage if player defeated during encounter) penalizes repeated attempts and forces players to clear additional stages before rematching, increasing commitment cost

faction_indicators: Evil Witch Doctor and persistent followers as antagonistic faction controlling islands
Monster minions as regional enforcers across eight islands
Four friendly dinosaurs as allied faction willing to aid Higgins against common oppressors
danger_signals: Boss relocation forcing extended island traversal as escalating threat response
Kidnapping of protagonist's girlfriend establishes existential danger narrative
Eight perilous islands under hostile control suggest widespread environmental danger
environmental_storytelling: Island theming implied through distinct stage types (underwater stages, vertical-scrolling stages) suggesting environmental variety and themed hazards
Dinosaur ability matching to stage mechanics (elasmosaurus for underwater, camptosaurus for ice) suggests environmental adaptation narrative
Checkpoint elimination contrasts with shorter stages, implying dangerous but navigable terrain requiring player mastery
